methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $141M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
MacroFab, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $141M to date, is evaluated against a universe of 9 same-sector peers spanning Series B through Series D (1 public, 8 private). Key comparables include 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C).

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x multiplier to MacroFab's $141M total raised, yielding $240M. Peer-median analysis across the 9-company set produces $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The latter reflects wide dispersion typical of early-stage robotics, with stage and commercial maturity driving significant valuation variance within the cohort.

Synthesizing both methods yields a valuation range of $204M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $920M. The stage-progression method anchors a conservative floor given MacroFab's position pre-later-stage funding. The peer-median method, while applicable, carries material uncertainty given the heterogeneity of comparable stages and the sector's nascent risk profile. Key sensitivities include revenue traction, unit economics, and competitive positioning relative to the named cohort—data not provided here.
